title: ATDD prompt asks each scenario to 'start from a running but empty system and share no data' — acceptance tests do the opposite
evidence: tests/test_acceptance.py:34-117 assert on real dataset values (e.g. Flamengo 2019 = 90 points, matches_played == 38); server.py:19-20 loads all CSVs into module-global state at import, shared across every test
suggestion: Either acknowledge that for a read-only fixed-dataset server 'empty system' is N/A, or seed each scenario from a small fixture dataset injected through the public interface so tests don't depend on the full production data.

title: Head-to-head and aggregate statistics can double-count matches via the un-deduplicated union
evidence: data_loader.py:68-78 get_all_matches() concats 5 frames with no dedup; BR-Football tags 'Serie A'->brasileirao and 'Copa do Brasil'->copa_do_brasil (data/kaggle/BR-Football-Dataset.csv), overlapping the dedicated CSVs; server.py:299 get_head_to_head and server.py:479 get_statistics query get_all_matches() unconditionally
suggestion: De-duplicate (date, home_team, away_team) when unioning, or route head-to-head/statistics through per-competition frames the way get_team_stats/get_standings already do.

title: No dependency manifest declaring runtime deps (mcp, pandas)
evidence: no requirements.txt / pyproject.toml / setup.py in run_dir; server.py:12 imports mcp.server.fastmcp and data_loader.py:6 imports pandas with nothing pinning them
suggestion: Add requirements.txt (or pyproject) pinning mcp and pandas so the server is reproducibly installable.

title: get_standings builds a points table for knockout competitions (Copa do Brasil, Libertadores)
evidence: server.py:381-388 accepts competition=copa_do_brasil/libertadores and accumulates a league-style points table, which is not meaningful for a cup/bracket
suggestion: Restrict standings to league competitions, or return a bracket/stage view for knockout competitions.

title: Date-range filtering compares against possibly-empty date strings
evidence: data_loader.py:29-37 _to_iso_date returns '' for unparseable dates; server.py:103-107 find_matches filters via string comparison df['date'] >= date_from, so '' rows are excluded from date_from but the empty-string sort ordering (server.py:109) is implicit
suggestion: Drop or flag rows with empty dates before date-range filtering/sorting so coverage is explicit.

title: Build + full test suite pass; 46 tests, 0 skips
evidence: scores.json test_coverage=1.0 (build+tests passed); 14 acceptance + 32 unit test functions; grep for pytest.skip/xfail in tests/ returned 0
